---
title: "01 - DePCR preprocessing"
author: "Luan Gaspar Clemente"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output:
  html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_depth: 3
    number_sections: true
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo = TRUE,
  eval = FALSE,
  message = FALSE,
  warning = FALSE
)
```

# Purpose

I use this document to describe the preprocessing workflow for the dePCR sequencing data. I download the SRA files, retrieve the SRA/SRR metadata, convert SRA files to FASTQ, merge paired-end reads, run quality control, summarize read counts, convert merged reads to FASTA/FNA, and generate the primer-template count matrix.

# 1. Download sequencing files

I download all sequencing files associated with the BioProject accession `PRJNA513137` and `PRJNA1072695` using `prefetch`.

```{bash}
prefetch PRJNA513137 -O path_to_files
prefetch PRJNA1072695 -O path_to_files

```

# 2. Retrieve SRA and SRR run metadata

I download the run information table from NCBI.

```{bash}
curl -L "https://trace.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/Traces/sra-db-be/runinfo?acc=PRJNA513137" \
  -o /mnt/c/files/sra_runinfo_PRJNA513137.csv
  curl -L "https://trace.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/Traces/sra-db-be/runinfo?acc=PRJNA1072695" \
  -o /mnt/c/files/sra_runinfo_PRJNA1072695.csv
```

# 3. Convert SRA files to FASTQ

I create the output folders for FASTQ files and temporary files. Then, I recursively search for `.sra` files and convert each one to FASTQ using `fasterq-dump`.

```{bash}
mkdir -p /mnt/c/files/fastq
mkdir -p /mnt/c/files/tmp

find /mnt/c/files -name "*.sra" -print0 |
while IFS= read -r -d '' file
do
  fasterq-dump "$file" \
    -O /mnt/c/files/fastq \
    -t /mnt/c/files/tmp \
    -e 8 \
    -p
done
```

# 4. Merge paired-end reads with VSEARCH

PEAR is no longer maintained as an open-source option, so I use VSEARCH to merge paired-end reads.

I create folders for merged outputs and log files. Then, I identify all forward reads ending in `_1.fastq`, find the matching reverse read ending in `_2.fastq`, and merge each pair.

```{bash}
mkdir -p /mnt/c/files/merged_vsearch
mkdir -p /mnt/c/files/merge_logs

for r1 in /mnt/c/files/fastq/*_1.fastq
do
  r2="${r1/_1.fastq/_2.fastq}"
  base=$(basename "$r1" _1.fastq)
  if [[ -f "$r2" ]]
  then
    echo "Merging Sample $base"
    vsearch \
      --fastq_mergepairs "$r1" \
      --reverse "$r2" \
      --fastqout "/mnt/c/files/merged_vsearch/${base}.assembled.fastq" \
      --fastqout_notmerged_fwd "/mnt/c/files/merged_vsearch/${base}.notmerged_R1.fastq" \
      --fastqout_notmerged_rev "/mnt/c/files/merged_vsearch/${base}.notmerged_R2.fastq" \
      --threads 4 \
      > "/mnt/c/files/merge_logs/${base}.vsearch.log" 2>&1
  else
    echo "ERROR: missing reverse read for $base"
  fi
done
```

# 5. Run FastQC and MultiQC

I run FastQC on the merged reads. Then, I use MultiQC to summarize all FastQC reports in a single report.

```{bash}
mkdir -p /mnt/c/files/fastqc_reports

fastqc /mnt/c/files/merged_vsearch/*.assembled.fastq \
  -o /mnt/c/files/fastqc_reports

source ~/multiqc_env/bin/activate

multiqc /mnt/c/files/fastqc_reports \
  -o /mnt/c/files/fastqc_reports
```

# 6. Count merged and unmerged reads

I summarize the number of merged and unmerged reads for each sample. 

I write one row per sample in the output file with the following columns:

- `sample`
- `merged`
- `not_merged_R1`
- `not_merged_R2`

```{bash}
echo -e "sample\tmerged\tnot_merged_R1\tnot_merged_R2" > /mnt/c/files/merge_summary.txt

for file in /mnt/c/files/merged_vsearch/*.assembled.fastq
do
  sample=$(basename "$file" .assembled.fastq)
  merged=$(($(wc -l < "/mnt/c/files/merged_vsearch/${sample}.assembled.fastq") / 4))
  notR1=$(($(wc -l < "/mnt/c/files/merged_vsearch/${sample}.notmerged_R1.fastq") / 4))
  notR2=$(($(wc -l < "/mnt/c/files/merged_vsearch/${sample}.notmerged_R2.fastq") / 4))
  echo -e "${sample}\t${merged}\t${notR1}\t${notR2}"
done >> /mnt/c/files/merge_summary.txt
```

# 7. Convert merged FASTQ files to FASTA/FNA

I convert the merged FASTQ files to FASTA format using `seqtk`. I save the converted files with the `.fna` extension.

```{bash}
mkdir -p /mnt/c/files/MergedFASTAFiles

for f in /mnt/c/files/merged_vsearch/*.assembled.fastq
do
  s=$(basename "$f" .assembled.fastq)
  seqtk seq -A "$f" > "/mnt/c/files/MergedFASTAFiles/$s.fna"
done
```

# 8. Count primer-template features

I create a Bash script to count primer-template features in the merged FNA files.

I use `list_templates.txt` as the feature annotation table. The script reads a file with columns similar to:

```text
feature    template    primer
```

For each sample, I count how many reads contain the expected primer-template pattern. I write the output table with samples as rows and features as columns.

```{bash}
nano ~/depcr/count_features.sh
```

Inside `count_features.sh`, I use:

```{bash}
#!/usr/bin/env bash

cd "$HOME/depcr/MergedFASTAFiles"

FEATURES="$HOME/depcr/list_templates.txt"
OUT="$HOME/depcr/FinalCount_samples_rows.tsv"

printf "sample" > "$OUT"

awk -F '\t' 'NR > 1 {gsub(/\r|"/,"",$1); printf "\t%s",$1} END {printf "\n"}' "$FEATURES" >> "$OUT"

for T in *.fna
do
    sample=$(basename "$T" .fna)
    echo "contando $sample"
    printf "%s" "$sample" >> "$OUT"
    awk -F '\t' 'NR > 1 {
        gsub(/\r|"/,"",$2)
        gsub(/\r|"/,"",$3)
        print $2 "\t" $3
    }' "$FEATURES" | while IFS="$(printf '\t')" read Template Primer
    do
        count=$(grep -Ec "$Primer.*$Template" "$T")
        printf "\t%s" "$count" >> "$OUT"
    done
    printf "\n" >> "$OUT"
done
```

After saving the script, I make it executable and run it.

```{bash}
chmod +x ~/depcr/count_features.sh
bash ~/depcr/count_features.sh
```

# 9. Main output files

This preprocessing workflow generates the following main outputs:

```text
/mnt/c/files/sra_runinfo_PRJNA513137.csv
/mnt/c/files/fastq/
/mnt/c/files/merged_vsearch/
/mnt/c/files/merge_logs/
/mnt/c/files/fastqc_reports/
/mnt/c/files/merge_summary.txt
/mnt/c/files/MergedFASTAFiles/
~/depcr/FinalCount_samples_rows.tsv
```

# 10. Notes

I do not trim primer sequences before counting because the primer identity is part of the feature definition. The count matrix depends on detecting the primer-template combination in each merged read.
